source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A very long conducting pipe has a square cross section of its inside surface, with side $D$ as in Fig. 1.47. Far from either end of the pipe is suspended a point charge located at the center of the square cross section. Determine the electric potential at all points inside the pipe, perhaps in the form of an infinite series.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $\varphi(x, y, z)= \frac{2 Q}{\pi \varepsilon_{0} D} \sum_{m, m^{\prime}=0}^{\infty} \frac{\cos\frac{(2m+1)\pi x}{D}\cos\frac{(2m^{\prime}+1)\pi y}{D}}{\sqrt{(2m+1)^2+(2m^{\prime}+1)^2}}\; e^{-\frac{x \mid 21}{D}\sqrt{(2m+1)^2+(2m^{\prime}+1)^2}}$

metadata.reasoning_chain:
Poisson's equation for the potential and the boundary conditions can be written as follows:
$$
\left. egin{array}{l}

abla^{2} \varphi=- rac{Q}{\varepsilon_{0}} \delta(x) \delta(y) \delta(z), \
\left.\varphiight|_{x=\pm D/2}=0, \
\left.\varphiight|_{y=\pm D/2}=0.
\end{array}ight\}
$$
By Fourier transform
$$
 ar{\varphi}(x,y,k)=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi(x,y,z)e^{ikz}dz
$$
the above become
$$
\left. egin{array}{l}
\left( rac{\partial^2}{\partial x^2}+ rac{\partial^2}{\partial y^2}-k^2ight) ar{\varphi}(x,y,k)=- rac{Q}{\varepsilon_{0}}\delta(x)\delta(y), \
\left. ar{\varphi}(x,y,k)ight|_{x=\pm D/2}=0, \
\left. ar{\varphi}(x,y,k)ight|_{y=\pm D/2}=0.
\end{array}ight\}
$$
A complete set of basis functions in the corresponding functional space is introduced and the delta function is expanded as
$$
\delta(x)\delta(y)=\left( rac{2}{D}ight)^2 \sum_{m,m^{\prime}=0}^{\infty} \cos rac{(2m+1)\pi x}{D}\cos rac{(2m^{\prime}+1)\pi y}{D}
$$
Assuming the general solution
$$
	ilde{\varphi}(x,y,k)=\sum_{m,m^{\prime}=0}^{\infty} ar{\varphi}_{mm^{\prime}}(k)\cos rac{(2m+1)\pi x}{D}\cos rac{(2m^{\prime}+1)\pi y}{D}
$$
and substituting into the previous equations, one finds
$$
 ar{\varphi}(x,y,k)= rac{4Q}{\varepsilon_{0}D^2} \sum_{m,m^{\prime}=0}^{\infty} rac{\cos rac{(2m+1)\pi x}{D}\cos rac{(2m^{\prime}+1)\pi y}{D}}{k^2+\left( rac{(2m+1)\pi}{D}ight)^2+\left( rac{(2m^{\prime}+1)\pi}{D}ight)^2}
$$
Applying the integral formula
$$
\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} rac{e^{ikz}}{k^2+\lambda^2} dk= rac{\pi}{\lambda}e^{-\lambda|z|},\quad (\lambda>0),
$$
we finally obtain
$$
\varphi(x,y,z)= rac{2Q}{\pi\varepsilon_{0}D} \sum_{m,m^{\prime}=0}^{\infty} rac{\cos rac{(2m+1)\pi x}{D}\cos rac{(2m^{\prime}+1)\pi y}{D}}{\sqrt{(2m+1)^2+(2m^{\prime}+1)^2}}\; e^{- rac{x \mid 21}{D}\sqrt{(2m+1)^2+(2m^{\prime}+1)^2}}.
$$
